title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE Biology Paper 2B - January 2020
subject: Biology

sub_questions.ms_text:
An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• (damage to) alveoli (1)
• reduces / less / small surface area (1)
• (less) diffusion / (less) oxygen in / (less) carbon dioxide out (1)

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:
• bacteria / microorganisms / pathogens are killed / destroyed / removed / eq (1)
• no / less chance of infection (with TB) / disease / catching Mycobacterium / Mycobacterium bovis / catching TB / eq (1)

An answer that makes reference to two of the following points:
• fewer cattle (to be infected) (1)
• fewer people in Europe (than in rest of world) (1)
• cattle are screened / if infected are killed (1)
• cattle are given antibiotics (1)
• vaccination (of cattle / people / badgers in Europe) (1)
• more pasteurised milk drunk / milk pasteurised (1)
• fewer badgers (in Europe to transmit TB) / fewer other animals (in Europe to transmit TB) / badgers culled (in Europe) (1)

An answer that makes reference to two of the following points:
• (find / protect / less competition for) mates / females (1)
• (protect) food / less competition for food / resources / obtain more food (1)
• protect young (1)

An explanation that makes reference to three of the following points:
• use attenuated / dead / weak / inactive pathogen / bacterium / eq (1)
• antigens (1)
• memory cells (1)
• secondary immune (response) (1)
• antibody production fast(er) / soon(er) / more / large amounts (1)

An answer that makes reference to three of the following points:
• vaccinate badgers in one area and not in another / eq (1)
• (count) number of cattle with TB / see if there are fewer / more cases of TB (1)
• same number / control number of badgers in each area (1)
• same field size / area (1)
• same number of cattle (1)

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:
• draws water up (to leaves) / enables water to be taken up (by roots) (1)
• cooling (1)
• (transport) minerals / named mineral (1)
• prevent wilting / eq (1)
• (water for) photosynthesis (1)

• reading from graph multiplied = 21. 5 x 10-4 = 0.00215
• multiply by 250
Answer: 0.5375 or 0.538 or 0.537 or 0.54 scores two (2)

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:
• wind moves water (away from plant surface) / blows water away (1)
• creates / maintains / increases (concentration) gradient (1)
• (more) (water moves out of plant by) diffusion / evaporation (1)

An answer that makes reference to the following points:
• (same) light (intensity) (1) as it affects stomata opening / eq (1)
OR
• (same) temperature (1) as it affects diffusion / speed of molecules / (kinetic) energy / evaporation (1)
OR
• humidity (1) reduces / affects concentration / diffusion gradient (1)
OR
• (same) species / age / size / mass / leaf area of plant (1) as this affects number of stomata / thickness of leaves / eq (1)
OR
• same CO2 concentration (1) as it affects stomata opening (1)
A the left ventricle is thick to pump blood to the body

An explanation that makes reference to three of the following points:
• ventricle (wall) is thinner / ventricle (wall) has less muscle (1)
• coronary artery is blocked / coronary artery has less blood passing through / eq (1)
• less oxygen / glucose (1)
• less (aerobic) respiration / anaerobic respiration occurs / not enough energy / less ATP (1)
• cell / muscle / tissue death (1)

An explanation that makes reference to three of the following points:
• (stem cells can) differentiate / can become muscle cells / can make any cell types / stem cells are undifferentiated / unspecialised cells / eq (1)
• stem cells divide / (carry out) mitosis / cell division (1)

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• cells have same DNA / genes / alleles / eq (1)
• same antigens / tissue type / blood group (1)
• less risk of rejection / not rejected (1)
• no immune response / by immune system / no need to take immunosuppressants (1)
• no risk of infection / disease spread (1)

An answer that makes reference to three of the following points:
• (DNA is) double stranded / helix (1)
• (DNA has) thymine / T / does not have uracil / U (1)
• (DNA has) deoxyribose (1)
• (DNA is) long(er) / large(r) (1)

A description that makes reference to five the following points:
• transcription occurs in the nucleus (1)
• transcription uses DNA to make RNA / eq (1)
• DNA unzips / one strand is copied / eq (1)
• transcription makes / produces messenger RNA / mRNA (1)
• translation takes place at the ribosomes / in cytoplasm (1)
• translation involves messenger RNA / mRNA and transfer RNA / tRNA (1)
• anticodons bind to codons / eq (1)
• translation produces amino acid chain / protein / polypeptide / eq (1)

• (if read in threes) = 4^3 or 4 x 4 x 4 (1)
• = 64 (1)
• (64 is) greater / more than 20 (combinations) required / needed / eq (1)
• (if read in twos) = 4^2 or 4 x 4 = 16 (1)
A collecting duct
D T (glomerulus)
A P (collecting duct)
B R (proximal convoluted tubule)

An answer that makes reference to the following points:
• increasing birth mass associated with more nephrons / eq (1)
• a positive correlation / linear / straight line / (directly) proportional (1)

An explanation that makes reference to four of the following points:
• proteins are big / large / only small molecules (1)
• normally will not pass out of glomerulus / through basement membrane / into Bowman’s capsule / not present in glomerular filtrate (1)
• (high blood pressure) forces / squeezes / pushes protein molecules out (1)
• damage to glomerulus / Bowman’s capsule / basement membrane / nephron (1)

• add Biuret (1)
• (changes to) purple/ lilac / mauve (1)

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• to mix microorganisms / bacteria / fungi with the food substrates / prevent microorganisms / food settling / eq (1)
• to mix / distribute / eq oxygen (1)
• to keep temperature uniform / distribute heat (1)

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:
• oxygen for respiration / for aerobic respiration (1)
• (filter) remove bacteria / fungi / microorganisms / prevent competition / contamination (1)

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• monitor temperature (in fermenter) / control / maintain temperature / detect changes in temperature / keep temperature same (1)
• turn on water supply to cooling jacket if temperature increases / turn off water supply to cooling jacket if temperature drops / eq (1)
• prevent enzymes being denatured / maintains optimal temperature for enzymes (1)
• Hh / Hh and Hh / hH / hH and hH / hH and Hh / Hh and hH (all heterozygous parents)

An answer that makes reference to two of the following points:
• small sample size / too few crosses / eq (1)
• random / chance (of fertilisation) (1)
• of fertilisation / fusion of gametes / eq (1)
• fewer yellow (than predicted) / more brown (than predicted) (1)
• no HH offspring survive / ratio is 2 Hh : yellow to 1 hh brown / yellow mice more likely die / brown mice less likely to die (1)
• mutations (may have occurred) (1)
